description: Azure Monitor dashboard for PostgreSQL Flexible Server, extended for ARO HCP troubleshooting. Includes deadlocks, longest query time, and failed connections metrics in addition to comprehensive PostgreSQL monitoring.

options.content:
## Azure Database for PostgreSQL - Monitoring Dashboard (V2)
#### Each metric is emitted at a 1-minute interval and has up to 93 days of history.
#### For complete list of metrics, refer [Azure PostgreSQL Monitoring Metrics](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/postgresql/flexible-server/concepts-monitoring)

panels.description: Major / Minor Version

azureResourceGraph.query:
// Fetches the PostgreSQL engine version (major.minor) configured on the server from control-plane metadata
resources
| where type =~ "microsoft.dbforpostgresql/flexibleservers"
| where name =~ "${res}"
| extend major = tostring(properties.version)
| extend minor = tostring(properties.minorVersion)
| project ["PostgreSQL Version"] = strcat(major, ".", minor)
| limit 1

panels.title: PostgreSQL version

panels.description: Server Region

azureResourceGraph.query:
// Retrieves the Azure region where the PostgreSQL flexible server resource is deployed
resources
| where type =~ "microsoft.dbforpostgresql/flexibleservers"
| where name =~ "${res}"
| project ["Region"] = tostring(location)
| limit 1

panels.title: Location

panels.description: SKU

azureResourceGraph.query:
// Returns the compute SKU associated with the PostgreSQL server (vCore and VM family configuration)
resources
| where type =~ "microsoft.dbforpostgresql/flexibleservers"
| where name =~ "${res}"
| project ["Server Config"] = tostring(sku.name)
| limit 1

panels.title: Compute size

panels.description: Indicates if the database is up or not
panels.title: Server State

panels.description: High Availability Configuration

azureResourceGraph.query:
// Determines whether High Availability is configured on the server using Resource Graph configuration properties
resources
| where type =~ "microsoft.dbforpostgresql/flexibleservers"
| where name =~ "${res}"
| extend haMode = tostring(properties.highAvailability.mode)
| extend haValue = iif(haMode =~ "Disabled" or isempty(haMode), 0, 1)
| project ["High-Availability"] = haValue
| limit 1

panels.title: High Availability

panels.description: Read Replica Configured

azureLogAnalytics.query:
//change this example to create your own time series query
<table name>                                                              //the table to query (e.g. Usage, Heartbeat, Perf)
| where $__timeFilter(TimeGenerated)                                      //this is a macro used to show the full chart’s time range, choose the datetime column here
| summarize count() by <group by column>, bin(TimeGenerated, $__interval) //change “group by column” to a column in your table, such as “Computer”. The $__interval macro is used to auto-select the time grain. Can also use 1h, 5m etc.
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Read-Replica

panels.title: T-Log Storage Used

panels.description: Amount of backup storage used. This metric represents the sum of storage that's consumed by all the full backups, differential backups, and log backups that are retained based on the backup retention period that's set for the server. The frequency of the backups is service managed. For geo-redundant storage, backup storage usage is twice the usage for locally redundant storage.

panels.description: Time of the longest currently running query. High values may indicate slow queries blocking cluster operations.
panels.title: Longest Query Time

panels.description: Number of failed connection attempts to the database. High values may indicate authentication issues or connection pool exhaustion.
panels.title: Failed Connections

options.content: #### **NOTE**: While metrics are stored for 93 days, you can only query (in the Metrics tile) for a maximum of 30 days' worth of data on any single chart. If you see a blank chart or your chart displays only part of metric data, verify that the difference between start and end dates in the time picker doesn't exceed the 30-day interval. After you've selected a 30-day interval, you can pan the chart to view the full retention window.

options.content: #### **NOTE**: Logs setup required. Metrics are available by default. PostgreSQL logs appear after configuring diagnostic settings to send logs to Azure Log Analytics. [Learn how to configure PostgreSQL logs](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/postgresql/monitor/how-to-configure-and-access-logs)

azureLogAnalytics.query:
// $__timeFilter(TimeGenerated) is a Grafana macro that expands to the dashboard's selected time range at query execution time
AzureDiagnostics
| where $__timeFilter(TimeGenerated)
| where ResourceProvider == "MICROSOFT.DBFORPOSTGRESQL"
| where ResourceGroup =~ "${rg}"  
| where Resource has "${res}"
| where Category == "PostgreSQLLogs"
| top 200 by TimeGenerated desc
| project Category, Message

panels.description: Shows the volume and frequency of PostgreSQL logs over time.

azureLogAnalytics.query:
// Log generation (derived from AzureDiagnostics) — follows Grafana $__interval
// Events       = number of PostgreSQL log records per interval
// MB_interval  = estimated log volume per interval (based on Message length)

AzureDiagnostics
| where $__timeFilter(TimeGenerated)
| where ResourceProvider == "MICROSOFT.DBFORPOSTGRESQL"
| where ResourceGroup =~ "${rg}"
| where Resource has "${res}"
| extend LogBytes = strlen(tostring(Message))
| summarize Events = count(), TotalBytes = sum(LogBytes) by bin(TimeGenerated, $__interval)
| extend MB_interval = todouble(TotalBytes) / (1024.0 * 1024.0)
| project TimeGenerated, Events, MB_interval
| order by TimeGenerated asc

panels.title: Log Generation Rate

panels.description: Number of transactions executed within a second.
panels.title: Transactions per second

options.content: #### **NOTE**: For autovacuum metrics to work, the server parameter `metrics.autovacuum_diagnostics` must be enabled. [Learn more about autovacuum monitoring](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/postgresql/monitor/concepts-monitoring#autovacuum-metrics)

panels.description: Estimated number of live rows for user-only tables in this database.
panels.title: Live Tuples

panels.description: Estimated number of dead rows for user-only tables in this database
panels.title: Dead Tuples

panels.description: Number of user-only tables that have been vacuumed by the autovacuum daemon in this database.
panels.title: User Tables AutoVacuumed

panels.description: Number of times user-only tables have been vacuumed by the autovacuum daemon in this database.

options.content: #### **NOTE**: For enhanced metrics to work, the server parameter `metrics.collector_database_activity` must be enabled. [Learn more about enhanced monitoring metrics](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/postgresql/monitor/concepts-monitoring#enhanced-metrics)

panels.description: Overall state of the back ends
panels.title: Sessions by State

panels.description: Sessions by the type of event for which the back end is waiting
panels.title: Sessions By WaitEventType

panels.description: Number of transactions in this database that have been committed
panels.title: Transactions Committed

panels.description: The Max Physical Replication Lag metric shows the lag in bytes between the primary and the most-lagging replica. This metric is applicable and available on the primary server only, and will be available only if at least one of the read replicas is connected to the primary. The lag information is present also when the replica is in the process of catching up with the primary, during replica creation, or when replication becomes inactive.
panels.title: Max Physical Replication Lag - On Primary

title: PostgreSQL Monitoring (ARO HCP Extended)
